title: Selecting platform in multiarch images is racy
body:
### Issue Description

For compatibility with docker podman appears to mark last used platform in multiarch images as default. I'm well aware of this, although not fan, as long as I can reliably select platform for new container explicitly. That however does not appear to be guaranteed. I often create 3 containers from same multiarch image: one for `linux/arm64` platform, one for `linux/arm/v6` and one for `linux/arm/v7`. Creation starts roughly at the same time and runs in parallel. Every now and then creation of one of the containers fails because the target platform was wrong. From a distance it looks like all 3 contend for setting default and use this default instead of keeping note what's the image to be used, setting it as default and then continue with marked image.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Start creation of multiple containers from same multiarch image targeting different platforms in parallel
2. Verify all containers have expected platform


### Describe the results you received

Intermittently some container has wrong platform

### Describe the results you expected

All containers have platform exactly as one explicitly requested.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

i can reproduce this. parallel creates from the same multi-arch tag with different  platform values occasionally select the wrong instance. this appears due to the mutable last-used platform becomes default behavior; concurrent pulls/creates race on that default, so an explicit platform on one request can be overridden by another.

workarounds:
- pin by digest: pull with the intended --platform, then create/run using name@sha256:<digest>
- or pull each platform to a distinct local tag (e.g., :arm64, :armv6, :armv7) and use those tags

@Luap99  whta u think about it ?

I don't think there is much we can do about this, for now this race is to be expected as the last write wins.

There is a lot more conversation in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/26621about this behavior so I close this in favour of that.

Using per platform digest should work as work around yes.

@Luap99 while these two issues are related they are somewhat different. I don't mind behavior that is raised in #26621 or that ultimate tag write is racy but is there no way for `podman create` to "write down" image id somewhere on the side prior to tag write? Passing `--platform linux/arm/v7` explicitly and getting container that is actually `linux/arm64` instead is quite bad.
